title: ceropegia volubicaulis
intro: Ceropegia volubicaulis, also known as the Twining Ceropegia, is a perennial vine with a climbing habit, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and funnel-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ceropegia volubicaulis are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and support respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
